choicesEnglish: An audio guide in Italian, for me.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! La richiesta standard alla cassa per adulti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo due ingressi a prezzo pieno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The standard request at the counter for adults.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for two full-price entries.

english: How much is the audio guide?
choicesEnglish: How much is the audio guide?
correctAnswerEnglish: How much is the audio guide?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere il prezzo del servizio di spiegazione vocale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere il costo dell'audioguida.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking the price for the vocal explanation service.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know the cost of the audio guide.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Cerchi il posto dove lasciare lo zaino.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando il deposito borse.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You're looking for the place to leave your backpack.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the bag storage.

choicesEnglish: I have the new ID card.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per avvisare che hai già acquistato i biglietti sul web.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi di aver riservato l'ingresso via internet.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To notify that you have already purchased tickets on the web.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that you've reserved entry via internet.

italian: Deve lasciare la borsa al guardaroba.
english: You must leave your bag at the cloakroom.
choicesItalian: Deve lasciare la borsa al guardaroba.
Deve mostrare il codice al tornello.
choicesEnglish: You must leave your bag at the cloakroom.
You must show the code at the turnstile.
You must touch the statue gently.
You must go up the stairs right away.
correctAnswerItalian: Deve lasciare la borsa al guardaroba.
correctAnswerEnglish: You must leave your bag at the cloakroom.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'istruzione comune per chi ha bagagli ingombranti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'obbligo di depositare la borsa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A common instruction for those with bulky luggage.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the requirement to deposit the bag.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Significa che non costa nulla.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica l'assenza di pagamento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! It means it costs nothing.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the absence of payment.

english: Where do I pick up the audio guides?
choicesEnglish: Where do I pick up the audio guides?
Where do I leave my backpack?
Where do I see the sculptures?
correctAnswerEnglish: Where do I pick up the audio guides?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere il punto di consegna dei dispositivi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere dove andare a prendere le audioguide.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking for the delivery point of the devices.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know where to go pick up the audio guides.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'informazione sulle agevolazioni tariffarie.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, specifica che chi studia ha lo sconto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Information on fare concessions.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it specifies that those who study have a discount.

english: I need to see the ID card.
choicesEnglish: I need to see the ID card.
correctAnswerEnglish: I need to see the ID card.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Il cassiere deve verificare il diritto allo sconto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la richiesta di mostrare il documento studentesco.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The cashier must verify the right to a discount.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the request to show the student document.

italian: In quale sala si trova il quadro?
english: In which room is the painting?
choicesItalian: In quale sala si trova il quadro?
In quale piazza si trova il bar?
In quale via si trova il museo?
In quale piano si trova l'uscita?
choicesEnglish: In which room is the painting?
In which square is the bar?
In which street is the museum?
On which floor is the exit?
correctAnswerItalian: In quale sala si trova il quadro?
correctAnswerEnglish: In which room is the painting?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per localizzare un'opera specifica.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando la stanza dove è esposto il dipinto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To locate a specific work.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the room where the painting is displayed.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una risposta precisa sulla posizione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica il numero della stanza dell'opera.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A precise answer on the location.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the room number of the work.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Bisogna salire le scale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica il livello superiore rispetto all'ingresso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You have to go up the stairs.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the upper level compared to the entrance.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere come salire al piano superiore.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando il modo per andare in alto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking how to go up to the upper floor.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are looking for a way to go up.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere il permesso per usare la fotocamera.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se è permesso fotografare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking permission to use the camera.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if it's allowed to take photos.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! È permesso fotografare ma con questa condizione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica il divieto di usare la luce artificiale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! It is allowed to photograph but with this condition.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the prohibition of using artificial light.

italian: Non è permesso toccare le sculture.
english: It is not allowed to touch the sculptures.
choicesItalian: Non è permesso toccare le sculture.
Non è permesso guardare i quadri.
Non è permesso entrare nel bar.
Non è permesso uscire dalla sala.
choicesEnglish: It is not allowed to touch the sculptures.
It is not allowed to look at the paintings.
It is not allowed to enter the bar.
It is not allowed to leave the room.
correctAnswerItalian: Non è permesso toccare le sculture.
correctAnswerEnglish: It is not allowed to touch the sculptures.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una regola di conservazione fondamentale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il divieto di contatto fisico con le opere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A fundamental conservation rule.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the prohibition of physical contact with the works.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un invito del guardiano a ridurre la distanza.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un modo educato per dire di venire più vicino.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A guard's invitation to reduce distance.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a polite way to say come closer.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Spiega perché non si deve toccare nulla.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla della fragilità delle opere d'arte.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Explains why you shouldn't touch anything.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it speaks of the fragility of the artworks.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un'indicazione per l'itinerario di visita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisce di rispettare la direzione stabilita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! An indication for the visit route.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it suggests following the established direction.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Significa che va pagata extra rispetto al biglietto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che non è inclusa nel prezzo d'ingresso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It means it must be paid extra compared to the ticket.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates it's not included in the entrance price.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere la durata di una visita o audioguida.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere l'estensione temporale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking the duration of a visit or audio guide.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know the time extension.

english: My phone is out of battery.
choicesEnglish: My phone is out of battery.
correctAnswerEnglish: My phone is out of battery.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un problema comune per i turisti tecnologici.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai segnalando che la batteria è finita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A common problem for tech tourists.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're reporting that the battery is dead.

english: We have earphones for one euro.
choicesEnglish: We have earphones for one euro.
We have tickets for ten euros.
We have guides for twenty euros.
We have bars for two euros.
correctAnswerEnglish: We have earphones for one euro.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'offerta di acquisto per ascoltare l'audioguida.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla del prezzo degli auricolari.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A purchase offer for listening to the audio guide.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about the price of the earphones.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere come ridare l'audioguida a fine visita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi informazioni sulla riconsegna del dispositivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking how to give back the audio guide at the end of the visit.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for info on returning the device.

english: At the exit, in the box.
choicesEnglish: At the exit, in the box.
At the entrance, in the bag.
At the register, in the hand.
At the bar, in the cup.
correctAnswerEnglish: At the exit, in the box.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! L'indicazione su dove lasciare l'audioguida.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, specifica il luogo e il contenitore per la restituzione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The indication on where to leave the audio guide.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it specifies the place and container for returning it.

english: I'm entitled to the reduced price, right?
choicesEnglish: I'm entitled to the reduced price, right?
I'm in a hurry to leave, right?
I'm afraid of the room, right?
correctAnswerEnglish: I'm entitled to the reduced price, right?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere conferma dello sconto (es. studenti).
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai verificando se puoi pagare meno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking for confirmation of the discount (e.g., students).
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are verifying if you can pay less.

english: You must show your ID card.
choicesEnglish: You must show your ID card.
You must run in the room.
correctAnswerEnglish: You must show your ID card.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'obbligo di esibire il documento scolastico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'istruzione per avere lo sconto studenti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The obligation to present the school document.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the instruction for getting the student discount.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Il motivo per cui vuoi usare il guardaroba.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, segnali un ingombro d'abbigliamento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The reason why you want to use the cloakroom.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're signaling bulky clothing.

choicesEnglish: It's on top of the roof.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'indicazione di posizione molto vicina.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che si trova svoltando l'angolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A very close position indication.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that it's found by turning the corner.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'augurio classico che lo staff fa ai turisti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il saluto per chi entra nel museo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The classic wish that staff gives to tourists.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the greeting for those entering the museum.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La domanda del cassiere sul metodo di pagamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla di come vuoi pagare i biglietti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The cashier's question about the payment method.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about how you want to pay for the tickets.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Indica che l'audioguida supporta più idiomi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce alla varietà linguistica del servizio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Indicates that the audio guide supports multiple idioms.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the service's linguistic variety.

italian: Italiano per me, inglese per mio marito.
english: Italian for me, English for my husband.
choicesItalian: Italiano per me, inglese per mio marito.
Pane per me, vino per mia sorella.
Biglietto per me, zaino per mia figlia.
Cassa per me, tornello per mio figlio.
choicesEnglish: Italian for me, English for my husband.
Bread for me, wine for my sister.
Ticket for me, backpack for my daughter.
Register for me, turnstile for my son.
correctAnswerItalian: Italiano per me, inglese per mio marito.
correctAnswerEnglish: Italian for me, English for my husband.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Specificare le preferenze linguistiche per le audioguide.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai scegliendo la lingua per due persone.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Specifying linguistic preferences for the audio guides.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are choosing the language for two people.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'indicazione su dove trovare qualcosa dopo l'ingresso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, specifica la posizione superata la barriera.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An indication on where to find something after the entrance.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it specifies the position past the barrier.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Confermare che si possono avere entrambe le lingue richieste.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la presenza di entrambe le opzioni.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Confirming that both requested languages are available.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the presence of both options.

english: You must give me a document (ID).
choicesEnglish: You must give me a document (ID).
You must give me a gift.
You must give me a kiss.
You must give me some help.
correctAnswerEnglish: You must give me a document (ID).

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! La richiesta del cassiere per verificare l'identità.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la richiesta della carta d'identità.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The cashier's request to verify identity.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the request for the ID card.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Indica che un servizio non è nel prezzo base.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che si paga separatamente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Indicates that a service is not in the base price.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means it is paid separately.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un riferimento spaziale per orientarsi nel museo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la posizione superata una determinata stanza.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A spatial reference to get oriented in the museum.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the position past a certain room.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un consiglio su cosa puoi portare nelle sale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisce di tenere solo bagagli di poco ingombro.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Advice on what you can carry into the rooms.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it suggests keeping only small-sized luggage.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per scusarsi di un piccolo errore commesso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'espressione tipica per chiedere scusa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To apologize for a small mistake made.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the typical expression to say sorry.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Spiegare la propria mancanza di comprensione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che non avevi afferrato il senso o la regola.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Explaining one's lack of understanding.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that you hadn't grasped the meaning or the rule.

english: The important thing is not to touch anymore.
choicesEnglish: The important thing is not to touch anymore.
The important thing is not to run anymore.
The important thing is not to shout anymore.
The important thing is not to leave anymore.
correctAnswerEnglish: The important thing is not to touch anymore.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'avviso del guardiano per il futuro.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, sottolinea la regola principale da rispettare d'ora in poi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The guard's warning for the future.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it emphasizes the main rule to be respected from now on.

english: I left it at the cloakroom.
choicesEnglish: I left it at the cloakroom.
I bought it at the register.
I saw it in the room.
I lost it in the square.
correctAnswerEnglish: I left it at the cloakroom.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Confermare di aver depositato il bagaglio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi la posizione della tua borsa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Confirming having deposited the luggage.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate your bag's location.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per dire che hai seguito le istruzioni.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica conformità a una domanda o regola.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To say that you followed the instructions.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates compliance with a request or rule.

english: I'll accompany you to the next room.
choicesEnglish: I'll accompany you to the next room.
I'll take you to the new street.
I'll call you to the register now.
I'll pay you at the guide immediately.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'll accompany you to the next room.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'offerta (o ordine) del guardiano di spostarsi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla di muoversi verso l'ambiente seguente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The guard's offer (or order) to move.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about moving towards the next environment.

italian: Il mio telefono è quasi scarico.
english: My phone is almost out of battery.
choicesItalian: Il mio telefono è quasi scarico.
Il mio biglietto è quasi perso.
Il mio zaino è quasi rotto.
Il mio tesserino è quasi vecchio.
choicesEnglish: My phone is almost out of battery.
My ID card is almost old.
correctAnswerItalian: Il mio telefono è quasi scarico.
correctAnswerEnglish: My phone is almost out of battery.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Segnalare un'imminente mancanza di energia del dispositivo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la batteria del cellulare sta per finire.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Signaling an imminent lack of power of the device.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the cell phone battery is about to run out.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere se si può usare l'audioguida a volume aperto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se servono per forza le cuffie.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking if you can use the audio guide with open volume.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if headphones are strictly necessary.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'istruzione su come usare l'audioguida.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica di digitare la cifra indicata vicino all'opera.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The instruction on how to use the audio guide.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates to type the number shown near the work.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Descrivere la collezione del museo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la presenza di opere celebri all'interno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Describing the museum's collection.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the presence of famous works inside.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per indicare celebrità universale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa ovunque.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To indicate universal fame.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means everywhere.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Decidere la quantità dell'acquisto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, confermi di volerne comprare due (es. audioguide).
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Deciding the purchase quantity.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you confirm you want to buy two (e.g., audio guides).

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'informazione piacevole per il portafoglio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che non costa soldi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Pleasant info for the wallet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates it doesn't cost money.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Cerchi il deposito borse.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo la posizione del guardaroba.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! You are looking for the bag storage.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for the location of the cloakroom.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! La domanda tipica alla cassa del museo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, riguarda la tipologia di biglietto da acquistare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The typical question at the museum counter.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it concerns the type of ticket to buy.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Consegnare il documento per lo sconto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai mostrando la tua tessera studentesca.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Handing over the document for the discount.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are showing your student card.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! L'informazione sul totale da pagare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, specifica la cifra in denaro richiesta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Information on the total to pay.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it specifies the amount of money requested.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un divieto molto importante in galleria.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, specifica che non si possono usare le mani sulle opere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A very important prohibition in the gallery.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it specifies that you cannot use your hands on the works.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! L'obbligo di deposito per bagagli ingombranti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla del dovere di lasciare le borse al guardaroba.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The mandatory deposit for bulky luggage.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it talks about the duty to leave bags at the cloakroom.

choicesEnglish: It's on top of the roof.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una specifica spaziale per trovare un servizio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica una posizione svoltando l'angolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A spatial specification for finding a service.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates a position by turning the corner.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un saluto cortese per chi inizia il tour.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai augurando piacere per il tempo al museo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A polite greeting for those starting the tour.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are wishing pleasure for the time at the museum.
